title: Black Pepper Nirvana
description: An earthy and tender Egyptian dish featuring whole black peppercorns that serves as a hangover cure for jain-friendly dining.

ingredients.name: Black pepper, freshly ground from whole peppercorns

instructions: Rinse and cook the brown rice with water until tender. Set aside.
In a large skillet, heat olive oil over medium heat. Add onions and garlic; sauté for about 5 minutes until translucent.
Add spinach leaves to the skillet; cook for an additional 2-3 minutes until wilted.
Add black-eyed peas, tomatoes, tahini sauce, lemon juice (if using), salt, and freshly ground pepper. Stir well to combine all ingredients.
Fold in cooked brown rice into the skillet mixture. Ensure everything is evenly distributed.

Transfer the dish to a slow cooker or an oven-safe pot if using a slow cooker, cover it, and cook for about 2 hours on low heat or 1 hour at 350°F (175°C) until heated through and flavors are well integrated.
